templates.description:
Re-read all four quarterly syntheses. Re-read your year's blog posts.
Look at your GitHub contribution graph. Write the year-in-review
document that captures the trajectory. This is also when you
reconsider the plan — the syllabus is allowed to evolve at year
boundaries far more than at month boundaries.
templates.title: Annual review (half a day)

templates.description:
30 min. Paper book, paper notebook, no laptop.
Same chair, same time. This is sacred.
Today's book: Computer Systems: A Programmer's Perspective.
templates.title: Morning reading: Computer Systems: A Programmer's Perspective

templates.description:
10–15 min. Whenever — commute, lunch, before bed.
Add 3–5 new cards per day from what you read and built. No more.
templates.title: Anki review

templates.description:
boot.dev, the side build, or the main project. In nvim.
Without AI writing code for you (using AI to *explain* concepts is fine).

Skipped on Sunday (rest day) and on pair days (the weekly
pair-with-engineer session replaces solo evening work that day).
templates.title: Evening hands-on (60–90 min)

templates.description:
One blog post or detailed notes document about something you learned
last month. 800–2000 words. Public, your real name, your domain.
Show your work — code samples, diagrams, real numbers. Don't optimize
for going viral; optimize for being useful in five years.
templates.title: Monthly post: write about last month

templates.description:
Once per quarter, pick a tool or system you use and plan a small
version of it from scratch. Time-box: 20–60 hours over 4–8 weeks.
The goal is not to build something useful — it's to demystify a
category of software. When done, write a blog post about what
surprised you.

Ideas (beyond the optional branches in the syllabus):
- toy DNS resolver (after Phase 1 networking — strongly recommended)
- toy git (init / add / commit; see Coglan's Building Git)
- toy load balancer
- toy ray tracer (one weekend with Peter Shirley's series)
- toy regex engine (Russ Cox's series)
- toy SQL query engine (after DDIA)
- CHIP-8 emulator

Today's task: pick the target, sketch the scope, set the time-box.
Don't start coding yet — synthesis comes first (the quarterly
synthesis task), then this.
templates.title: Build the thing under the thing — pick the next target

templates.description:
2–3 hrs. Re-read three months of journal entries. Re-read three
months of monthly retrospectives. Write a single page: what changed
about how you think? What can you now do that you couldn't 90 days
ago? What's still confusing? This page is the most valuable single
artifact you produce that quarter.
templates.title: Quarterly synthesis

templates.description:
Without looking at your notes, write down the 3 most important
things you learned this week. Then compare to your notes. The gap
is the diagnostic — that's what your Anki cards for next week
should target.
templates.title: Friday review: 20-min retrieval

templates.description:
Phase 1, ~60 hrs. boot.dev courses 1–7 (Python, Linux, Git, OOP,
Functional). Skim chapters where you already know the material;
do every exercise where you don't. boot.dev is your runway, not
your syllabus. Move fast through this.
templates.title: Module 1: Python Basics — start
